back_translation: The ark of the LORD was in the land of the Philistines seven months.
The Philistines called the priests and the diviners, saying, 'What shall we do with the ark of the LORD? Let us know with what we shall send it to its place.'

They said, 'If you are sending the ark of the God of Israel, do not send it empty, for returning you shall return to him a guilt offering — there you will be healed, and it will be known to you why his hand does not turn from you.'

They said, 'What is the guilt offering that we shall return to him?' They said, 'The number of the rulers of the Philistines — five tumors of gold and five mice of gold — for one plague is to all of you and to your rulers.'

'You shall make images of your tumors and images of your mice that are destroying the land — and you shall give to the God of Israel glory; perhaps he will lighten his hand from upon you and from upon your gods and from upon your land.'
'And why will you harden your hearts as the Egyptians and Pharaoh hardened theirs? Was it not when he had wrought against them, that they sent them away and they went?'
'And now, take and make one new cart and two milk cows upon which no yoke has come, and you shall bind the cows to the cart, and you shall return their calves from behind them to the house.'

'And you shall take the ark of the LORD and set it on the cart, and the objects of gold that you are returning to him as a guilt offering you shall set in the box from beside it — and you shall send it and it shall go.'

'And you shall see — if it goes up the way of its territory to Beth-shemesh, he is the one who did to us this great evil; and if not, then we shall know that his hand did not touch us — chance happened to us.'
The men did so, and they took two milk cows and bound them to the cart, and their calves they shut up in the house.
They put the ark of the LORD on the cart, and the box, and the mice of gold and the images of their tumors.

The cows went straight on the way, on the way to Beth-shemesh — on one highway they went, lowing as they went, and they did not turn right or left, and the rulers of the Philistines were going after them as far as the border of Beth-shemesh.
The Beth-shemeshites were reaping the wheat harvest in the valley, and they lifted up their eyes and saw the ark, and they rejoiced to see it.

The cart came to the field of Joshua the Beth-shemeshite and stood there, and there was a great stone, and they split the wood of the cart, and the cows they offered up as a burnt offering to the LORD.

The Levites brought down the ark of the LORD and the box that was with it, in which were the objects of gold, and they set them on the great stone — and the men of Beth-shemesh offered up burnt offerings and sacrificed sacrifices on that day to the LORD.
The five rulers of the Philistines saw, and they returned to Ekron on that day.
These are the tumors of gold that the Philistines returned as a guilt offering to the LORD: for Ashdod one, for Gaza one, for Ashkelon one, for Gath one, for Ekron one.

And the mice of gold — the number of all the cities of the Philistines of the five rulers, from a fortified city to a village of the countryside — and as far as Abel-the-great upon which they set the ark of the LORD, unto this day in the field of Joshua the Beth-shemeshite.

He struck the men of Beth-shemesh because they looked in the ark of the LORD, and he struck among the people seventy men — fifty thousand men. The people mourned, because the LORD struck among the people a great striking.
The men of Beth-shemesh said, 'Who is able to stand before the LORD, this holy God? And to whom shall he go up from upon us?'
They sent messengers to the inhabitants of Kiriath-jearim, saying, 'The Philistines have returned the ark of the LORD — come down, bring it up to you.'
